summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the sermon by acknowledging the universal human experience of neediness, from healing and breakthroughs to guidance, comfort, and freedom. He framed this not as a weakness but as a God-given design, leading into a deep exploration of Psalm 23. Pastor Travis emphasized that the declaration 'The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want' is a profound theological truth from David, a former shepherd and king who understood God's personal provision, guidance, and protection. The central message, titled 'My God Shall Supply,' was anchored in Philippians 4:19. Pastor Travis broke down this promise into four key understandings: God knows what we need better than we do, He knows the perfect timing for that need, He understands the greater purpose behind why we need it (or don't receive it), and He has a customized 'how' for providing it, often in unexpected ways. He illustrated these points with personal testimonies of recent health challenges within his family and his own recovery from a stroke, framing them within the context of spiritual warfare and God's sovereign timing. The sermon concluded with a powerful call to trust in God as our sole source and shepherd, to persevere in faith, and to surrender our lives to Christ, culminating in a moment of prayer and praise.
mainPoints: God knows what we need better than we know ourselves, and He promises to supply all our needs according to His riches.
God's timing is perfect and purposeful; what we perceive as a delay is often His divine design for our ultimate good.
God has a customized plan for how He provides, and we must trust His methods even when they are unexpected or require perseverance.

versesMentioned.text: The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing.
Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.
When Jesus spoke again to the people, he said, 'I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.'
And my God will meet all your needs according to the riches of his glory in Christ Jesus.

Jesus answered, 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again, but whoever drinks the water I give them will never thirst. Indeed, the water I give them will become in them a spring of water welling up to eternal life.'
Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.
And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose.

'For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways,' declares the Lord. 'As the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts.'

Monday.scripture: Psalm 23:1 (NIV) - 'The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing.'
Monday.action: Identify one area where you feel 'needy' today. Write it down and consciously choose to declare, 'The Lord is my shepherd in this; I lack nothing.'
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for being my shepherd. Help me to trust today that in you, I truly lack nothing. Shift my focus from my need to your provision.

Tuesday.scripture: Philippians 4:19 (NIV) - 'And my God will meet all your needs according to the riches of his glory in Christ Jesus.'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on a past prayer God answered in an unexpected way or in His perfect timing. Thank Him for His wisdom in that situation.
Tuesday.prayer: God, I claim your promise to supply all my needs. Forgive me for trying to be my own provider. I trust your riches and your timing today.

Wednesday.scripture: John 4:13-14 (NIV) - '...whoever drinks the water I give them will never thirst.'
Wednesday.action: Evaluate one 'well' you go to for comfort or satisfaction (e.g., social media, entertainment, food). Intentionally spend time in prayer or Scripture instead.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, you are the living water. I confess I often drink from wells that leave me thirsty. Satisfy my soul today with your presence and your word.

Thursday.scripture: Galatians 6:9 (NIV) - '...at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.'
Thursday.action: Is there a prayer you've nearly given up on? Write it on a card and place it somewhere you'll see it as a reminder to not grow weary.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I choose not to give up. I trust that you are working, and at the proper time, I will see the harvest. Strengthen my faith to wait on you.

Friday.scripture: Romans 8:28 (NIV) - 'And we know that in all things God works for the good...'
Friday.action: Think of a current difficulty. Practice saying, 'God, I don't understand this, but I trust that you are working it for my good and your purpose.'
Friday.prayer: Lord, your ways are higher than mine. Help me to trust your 'why' even when I can't see it. Work this situation for my good and your glory.

Saturday.scripture: Isaiah 55:8-9 (NIV) - '...my ways are higher than your ways...'
Saturday.action: Read one of the biblical 'how' stories mentioned (e.g., Peter's coin in the fish, Naaman's healing). Meditate on how creative God is.
Saturday.prayer: God, you see the full picture. I surrender my expectations for how you should answer my prayers. I am open and expectant for your customized, miraculous 'how.'
